    Coins.js

    Overview

    An arcade coin machine game made with the three.js javascript library.

    Development

    I’ve always loved those 2p coin machine cabinets you find at the amusements. I’ve sunk a small fortune in to them over the years. I thought it would be a good simple project to show off my prowess with the three.js library.

    I used Physijs for the physics. It’s super easy to incorporate as it uses the same conventions as three.js, and it works great.

    I could perhaps have sourced some free models for the cabinet and coins, but I love fiddling around in Blender, so I set out to make them myself. I kept the models simple so that they would not be too taxing. I then used the three.js Blender Export addon to convert the models to JSON format.

    I wanted some nice coin sound effects and some arcade background ambience to make things more immersive, so I looked on freesound.org. I found a nice loopable ambience track that fit perfect. For the coins, I found one track of somebody dropping some coins on to a metal surface, and chopped it up in to several sound effects with Audacity. For the gradient background, I found a public domain photo of an amusements interior and added a whole lot of gaussian blur with paint.net.

    Three.js has built-in anti-aliasing to combat those jagged edges, but I found it to be too taxing on modest hardware when applied to my game, so instead I opted for a SMAA technique…better performance, and still looks rather nifty! For this I used the SMAA shader HERE.

    I hope to eventually polish this up and turn in to more of a fleshed out game…when I have time, of course!

    Download

    The GitHub repository for the project is HERE.
